topic: Latent-realizability of proximal bridge-null directions. Pre-anchor check: closest anchors are proximal causal inference without unique bridges, the published null-annihilator quotient criterion, and finite negative-control latent-class formulations.

open_problems.open_problem: Characterize the cone of proximal bridge-null directions in a finite observed negative-control law that are induced by valid latent negative-control causal models.

source_refs.claim: Bridge-equation solutions may be nonunique, complicating proximal estimation and inference, while a counterfactual mean can still be identified as a functional.
source_refs.author: Zhang, Li, Miao, and Tchetgen Tchetgen

source_refs.claim: Categorical double-negative-control identification uses finite latent/confounder and negative-control structure with weaker conditions than generic completeness.
source_refs.author: Shi, Miao, Nelson, and Tchetgen Tchetgen

source_refs.excerpt: every rational point of the observable bridge envelope is realized
preserves the full observed Y law

open_problems.why_unsolved: The literature separates two ingredients but does not connect them: Zhang et al. treat nonunique bridge solution sets and functional invariance, while finite categorical negative-control work gives latent proxy structures under rank/invertibility conditions. Prior CausalSmith reviewers identified the missing step as a finite realization theorem: observed bridge-fiber arithmetic is not enough unless the perturbed fiber can be completed to a nonnegative latent law preserving the observed outcome and proxy restrictions.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt to identify the realizable cone with the bridge operator kernel fails because the prior reviewer demanded preservation of the full observed outcome law and Shi et al.'s categorical model imposes nonnegative stochastic latent-proxy structure beyond the observed linear equation.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A necessary-and-sufficient finite response-type theorem or Farkas-style certificate for when a bridge-null vector can be lifted to a latent negative-control causal law, plus a counterexample for a non-liftable algebraic null vector.

open_problems.open_problem: Give sharp ATE bounds in finite rank-deficient proximal negative-control models after restricting bridge-null perturbations to latent-realizable directions rather than all algebraic bridge fibers.

source_refs.claim: When completeness fails, proxies may still yield partial-identification bounds even when neither a bridge function nor the causal effect is point identified.
source_refs.author: Ghassami, Shpitser, and Tchetgen Tchetgen

source_refs.claim: Double negative controls identify causal effects through an outcome confounding bridge and negative-control exposure under proxy relevance conditions.
source_refs.author: Miao, Shi, Li, and Tchetgen Tchetgen

source_refs.excerpt: give the exact ATE or mediation-effect identified set
finite realization of every proxy-null array was missing

open_problems.why_unsolved: Ghassami et al. deliberately bypass bridge identification to obtain observed-data partial-identification bounds, while bridge-based proximal papers assume enough proxy relevance for identification. The CausalSmith prior ledger records the unresolved middle regime: finite rank-deficient bridge operators produce algebraic intervals, but endpoint sharpness depends on whether the endpoint directions are latent-model realizable.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt to optimize the ATE over all observed-data bridge solutions fails because Ghassami et al. replace unidentified bridges with different nonsmooth bounds and the reviewer record says proxy-null endpoint arrays still require a finite realization proof.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A sharp finite-dimensional identified-set theorem whose endpoints solve an LP or polynomial program with explicit latent-completion witnesses and dual infeasibility certificates for nonrealizable algebraic endpoints.

open_problems.open_problem: Construct a non-equivalence witness: two finite observed laws with the same bridge-operator nullspace and the same quotient/null-annihilator certificate but different sharp ATE sets because their null directions differ in latent realizability.

source_refs.claim: The published nonunique-bridge result resolves the algebraic row-space/null-annihilator criterion for functional identification, not latent sharp-set equivalence.
source_refs.author: Zhang, Li, Miao, and Tchetgen Tchetgen

source_refs.claim: Finite categorical negative-control models impose latent-class and negative-control constraints that are stronger than an observed bridge matrix nullspace alone.
source_refs.author: Shi, Miao, Nelson, and Tchetgen Tchetgen

source_refs.excerpt: already give the bridge-nullspace orthocomplement criterion
latent negative-control causal models rather than merely by algebraic bridge fibers

open_problems.why_unsolved: The quotient/null-annihilator problem itself is banked as already solved, so the live gap must separate algebraic bridge fibers from causal latent fibers. Published and in-repo evidence both point to this distinction but do not give a pair of observed finite laws showing that identical nullspace certificates can mask different sharp causal identified sets.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt to reuse the Zhang-Li-Miao-Tchetgen null-annihilator certificate fails because the banked rejection says that certificate is only algebraic, while Shi et al.'s finite model requires a valid latent negative-control law behind the same observed matrix.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A hand-checkable 2-by-3 or 3-by-3 finite witness pair with identical bridge operator kernels and quotient certificates, explicit latent completions for one law, and a dual/nonnegativity obstruction for the other.

open_problems.open_problem: Find observable algebraic necessary conditions for finite latent-class realizability of a bridge-null direction beyond row-rank, support, and standard completeness failures.

source_refs.claim: Proxy-variable causal identification can hold with two conditionally independent proxies and rank conditions even when the full latent measurement model is not identified.
source_refs.author: Miao, Geng, and Tchetgen Tchetgen

source_refs.claim: Practical proximal analyses rely on difficult-to-verify assumptions about negative-control validity and completeness tied to a plausible unmeasured confounder.
source_refs.author: Zivich, Cole, Edwards, Mulholland, Shook-Sa, and Tchetgen Tchetgen

source_refs.excerpt: image of latent completions paired with an explicit h
open-class finite latent nonrealizability of bridge

open_problems.why_unsolved: Foundational proxy-variable papers use rank/relevance assumptions to secure identification or weaker finite categorical formulas, and applied proximal guidance emphasizes that assumptions are hard to verify from observables. The reviewer trail isolates a more refined diagnostic problem: define conditions visible in the finite observed law that rule in or rule out a proposed null direction as the image of a latent completion paired with an explicit bridge.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt to use row-rank or support positivity as the diagnostic fails because Miao et al. show causal parameters may be identified even when the full latent model is not, while the reviewer asks for the image of latent completions rather than a matrix-only condition.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A finite algebraic certificate, preferably a small determinant/minor plus inequality system or its Farkas dual, giving testable necessary conditions and a proven gap from standard rank/completeness checks.

open_problems.open_problem: Classify minimal 2-by-2, 2-by-3, or 3-by-3 negative-control tables that separate unique bridge, quotient-identified nonunique bridge, latent-realizable partial identification, and spurious algebraic bridge fibers.

source_refs.excerpt: finite examples exhibiting all three phases
without a valid finite observed law

open_problems.why_unsolved: Prior proposals repeatedly used small examples, but reviewers flagged them as either matrix-only or invalid finite laws. The useful open problem is not another isolated example but a minimal taxonomy with valid observed laws, bridge equations, latent completions, and nonrealizability certificates for each phase.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt to exhibit any rank-deficient matrix fails because the reviewer evidence rejects matrix-only witnesses without a valid finite observed law and endpoint certificates.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A complete small-support classification theorem with explicit tables, bridge operators, ATE loadings, latent laws or dual obstructions, and a proof that the listed phases exhaust the minimal cases.

literature_map:
1. Miao-Geng-Tchetgen Tchetgen (2018, Biometrika, miao2018proxyVariables) is the finite/proxy identification anchor: two proxies plus rank-style restrictions can identify causal effects even when the latent measurement model itself is not fully identified.
2. Miao-Shi-Li-Tchetgen Tchetgen (2024, Statistical Theory and Related Fields, miao2018confoundingBridge) formalizes outcome bridge functions and double negative-control identification.
3. Shi-Miao-Nelson-Tchetgen Tchetgen (2020, JRSSB, shi2020multiplyRobustDNC) gives categorical double-negative-control formulas, the closest published finite latent-class substrate.
4. Cui-Pu-Shi-Miao-Tchetgen Tchetgen (2023/2024, JASA, cui2024semiparametricProximal) develops semiparametric proximal ATE theory and notes future partial-ID directions when completeness is only approximate.
5. Zhang-Li-Miao-Tchetgen Tchetgen (2023, Statistics & Probability Letters, zhang2023proximalNonunique) is the already-solved algebraic nonunique-bridge/functional-identification anchor.
6. Ghassami-Shpitser-Tchetgen Tchetgen (2024, arXiv, ghassami2023partialProxy) supplies partial-ID bounds without completeness, but not latent-realizable bridge-null cones.
7. Kallus-Mao-Uehara (2022, arXiv, kallus2021minimaxNegativeControls) and Bennett et al. weak-functional work explain estimator behavior under nonunique/weak nuisance functions, not causal latent-fiber realizability.
8. Zivich et al. (2023, AJE, zivich2023introducingPCI) underscores the practical obstacle: negative-control validity and completeness are difficult to justify without a credible latent confounder.
9. The live gap is therefore not the null-annihilator quotient criterion or generic LP/Manski bounds; it is the finite causal-realizability layer between observed bridge fibers and latent negative-control laws.
prior_proposal_map:
1. Current research/pid_proximal_null_realizability has only state/pipeline stubs; no active proposal or reviews were available.
2. _bank/failed/eid_proximal_bridge_quotient_v1 was rejected as already solved by the Zhang et al. null-annihilator criterion, but its suggested directions explicitly name finite witness classification and latent-model-induced null directions.
3. _bank/downgraded/eid_proximal_phase_v1 supplies the richest prior ledger: reviewers asked for finite realization, endpoint LP certificates, explicit support-size bounds, and examples exhibiting all phases.
4. _bank/failed/eid_proximal_completeness_v1 reviews repeatedly reject matrix-only witnesses and ask for full finite observed laws or latent-completion images.
5. pid_mediation_proxy and related proxy-envelope entries preserve the same sharpness obstruction: LP/projection arithmetic is not enough without endpoint-attaining latent causal completions.
